title: Deployment Config
description: Saved per-deployment configuration for the daily-reporting plugin. Machine-readable mirror of references/schemas/deployment-config-schema.md. See ./MAINTAINING.md for the markdown <-> JSON Schema sync discipline.
$comment: Top-level shape per deployment-config-schema.md: version, plugin_name, deployment_name, deployment_mode, enabled_modes, defaults, source_map, connector_settings, validation_status. The 'Minimum valid deployment' checklist requires enabled_modes, timezone (inside defaults), participants (inside defaults), continuity_store/delivery_policy/review_policy (inside defaults).

schema_version.description: Schema version identifier. See ./MAINTAINING.md for the versioning policy and sync discipline.

version.description: Deployment-config document version string used by tooling to tag stored config snapshots.

plugin_name.description: Name of the plugin this config targets (typically 'daily-reporting').

deployment_mode.description: Declares whether the deployment uses live connectors or runs without them.
deployment_mode.$comment: connected: all enabled sources are served by live connectors. manual_only: no connectors wired; deployment runs from operator input and prior state only. disconnected: connectors wired but temporarily not used (degrade to manual fallbacks).

enabled_modes.description: Ordered list of which reporting modes are active for this deployment.
enabled_modes.$comment: Required per the 'Minimum valid deployment' checklist: at least one mode must be enabled. Each entry must be one of 'sod' or 'eod'.

defaults.description: Default values applied to every run for this deployment.
defaults.$comment: Per 'Required defaults': defaults should include timezone, participants, continuity_store, delivery_policy, review_policy, retrieval_policy, retrieval_windows.

timezone.description: IANA timezone string used to compute report_date for this deployment.
timezone.$comment: Required per 'Minimum valid deployment'. See runtime-input-schema.md 'Timezone edge cases' for DST and override handling.

participants.description: Canonical participant declaration. Other schemas reference this shape by pointer.

source_map.description: Per-source-family configuration keyed by the six canonical source keys.
source_map.$comment: Keyed by the canonical source keys declared in 'Source keys': calendar, email, tasks, meetings, prior_state, manual. Each entry may declare enabled, provider, input_domains, authority, and (for tasks) scope_rule.

connector_settings.description: Per-connector configuration keyed by connector name.
connector_settings.$comment: Connector names vary by provider. Recognized names in the starter template: google-calendar, outlook-calendar, gmail, executive-workflow, meeting-notes, prior-state, manual. Every entry may declare 'enabled' plus optional 'budget' (v1.1 cluster 2) and 'filtering_overrides' (v1.1 cluster 3). gmail additionally may declare high_signal_labels and vip_senders.

configured.description: True when the deployment has completed setup validation.

last_validated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last validation run.

blocking_issues.description: Ordered list of unresolved blocking issues; empty when deployment is valid.

owner_type.description: Role identifier used for permission checks and the confirmed_role vocabulary.
owner_type.$comment: Vocabulary rule: wherever a role is referenced by identifier (not participant name) - including review_policy.can_review, review_policy.can_confirm, and structured_state.confirmed_role - use this vocabulary. Do not use 'executive'; use 'exec'.

participant.description: Canonical participant shape. Referenced by pointer from runtime-input, normalized-context, and output schemas.

name.description: Human-readable label used in reports and structured_state.confirmed_by matches.

continuity_store.description: Declares where prior locked reports and structured state live so the next cycle can seed from them.

type.description: Continuity backend identifier. Current canonical value: 'file_archive'.

human_readable_reports_path.description: Relative or absolute path where locked human-readable reports are written.

structured_state_path.description: Relative or absolute path where locked structured-state artifacts are written.

allow_draft_continuity.description: When true, draft (unlocked) reports may seed continuity; when false, only locked reports do.
allow_draft_continuity.$comment: See ../policies/continuity-model.md. Safe-posture default is false (v1.1 cluster 1).

delivery_policy.description: Declares where drafts and locked finals are sent.

draft_destination.description: Identifier of the surface that receives generated drafts and revisions awaiting review.

final_destination.description: Identifier of the surface that receives locked finals.

send_revisions_to_review_surface.description: When true, in-review revisions are re-sent to the draft/review surface.

send_only_locked_finals_to_final_destina.description: When true, only locked reports reach final_destination.

retrieval_policy.description: Declares the default behavior connectors should assume when the deployment runs.

use_delegated_retrieval_when_available.description: When true, connector families may fan out work via the host runtime's delegated-retrieval primitive.

prefer_normalized_summaries.description: When true, connectors must return normalized per-family summaries (the shapes in normalized-context-schema.md) rather than raw payloads.

allow_raw_transcripts_by_default.description: When true, meeting and email connectors may include raw transcript text; normally false.

executive_scope_only.description: When true, connectors must exclude non-executive-owned items from retrieval by default.

retrieval_window_value.$comment: The retrieval window vocabulary is per-family. See deployment-config-schema.md 'Retrieval windows' for the canonical enum per source family.

retrieval_windows_by_mode.description: Per-source retrieval window selection for a single mode.

retrieval_windows.description: Default retrieval window for each source family, keyed by mode.
retrieval_windows.$comment: Canonical prose form lives in ../policies/source-windows.md; this schema enumerates the canonical string values.

review_policy_fields_properties.description: Properties-only definition of the 12 review_policy fields. Used both as the base review_policy shape (via allOf) and — when wrapped in a strict variant — as the override subset for mode_overrides.sod/eod.

finalization_mode.description: Which finalization policy the deployment applies.
finalization_mode.$comment: Safe-posture default is 'reviewed_locked' (v1.1 cluster 1). 'auto_locked' is gated by runtime run-history (see review-and-locking.md 'Deployment posture').

can_review.description: Roles allowed to review. Must use owner_type vocabulary.
can_review.$comment: Every entry must correspond to the owner_type of at least one participant. See review-and-locking.md 'Reviewer authority resolution'.

can_confirm.description: Roles allowed to confirm. Must use owner_type vocabulary and correspond to a declared participant's owner_type.

material_edits_require_reconfirmation.$comment: See ../policies/review-and-locking.md 'Material vs. non-material edits' for the canonical definition.

review_policy_override.$comment: Per-mode override object: any subset of the 12 review_policy fields, strict no-unknowns.

review_policy.description: Full review policy; required base, plus an optional mode_overrides block.

mode_overrides.description: Optional per-mode override of any subset of the 12 review_policy fields above.
mode_overrides.$comment: Out of scope for v1: the starter template and connected example do not ship this block. Declared here for forward compatibility only.

authority_enum.description: Declares the family's weight when merge rules combine sources.
authority_enum.$comment: Ordering: authoritative > supporting > fallback. 'manual' is the canonical fallback.

scope_rule_tasks_enum.description: Narrows which items a tasks connector may return.
scope_rule_tasks_enum.$comment: tasks is the only family for which a scope_rule is required in v1. 'executive_workflow_only' excludes non-executive-owned items.

source_map_entry.description: Shape of a single source_map.<family> entry (non-tasks family).

input_domains.description: Always an array of strings; use a one-element array when the family contributes a single domain.

source_map_entry_tasks.description: Shape of source_map.tasks — requires scope_rule in v1.
source_map_entry_tasks.$comment: 'tasks must remain explicitly executive-scoped, for example scope_rule: executive_workflow_only.' If a connector setting for the same provider also accepts a scope_rule, the two values must agree.

connector_entry_base_props.description: Fields every connector entry may declare.

enabled.description: When false, the connector is skipped even if its source family is enabled in source_map.

budget.description: Per-run retrieval budget override for this connector.
budget.$comment: v1.1 cluster 2 addition. See ../policies/retrieval-budgets.md 'Override mechanism' for defaults and behavior.

filtering_overrides.description: Per-connector override of default noise-exclusion or signal-prioritization rules.
filtering_overrides.$comment: v1.1 cluster 3 addition. See ../policies/source-filtering.md 'Override mechanism' for shape and behavior.

connector_entry_generic.$comment: Catch-all connector entry shape. google-calendar, outlook-calendar, and manual use only 'enabled' in the starter template, plus the optional budget and filtering_overrides.

high_signal_labels.description: gmail labels treated as high-signal for prioritization (e.g., ['Needs Action', 'Urgent', 'Important', 'VIP']).
high_signal_labels.$comment: v1.1 cluster 3 addition. See ../policies/source-filtering.md (email signal prioritization).

vip_senders.description: Email addresses or domains treated as VIP for prioritization.

connector_entry_executive_workflow.description: Connector for the tasks source family.

include_support_work_only_if_exec_releva.description: When true, support-layer items surface only when they materially affect executive execution.

connector_entry_meeting_notes.description: Connector for the meetings source family.

unresolved_only.description: When true, the connector returns only unresolved items from the configured retrieval window.

action_relevant_only.description: When true, the connector filters to action/followup-bearing items.

connector_entry_prior_state.description: Connector for the prior_state source family.

prefer_structured_state.description: When true, the connector reads structured_state_path in preference to human-readable reports.

allow_draft_continuity.description: When true, draft (unlocked) prior state may seed this cycle; mirror of continuity_store.allow_draft_continuity.
